metric: Size of New York state statutory law in force, annual snapshots
provenance: GET /api/3/laws/{lawId}?date=YYYY-MM-DD&full=true for every law in /api/3/laws, at 1 January 2015-2026. Sections counted as tree nodes with docType=='SECTION', deduplicated by locationId. Words are whitespace tokens over each section's text field. Key read from macOS Keychain, never logged. Responses cached; backoff on 429/5xx.
reason: Passes the coverage-artifact checks that the NYC Administrative Code failed. (1) All-laws growth and fixed-cohort growth agree to within 0.005 (words 1.1148 vs 1.1101; sections 1.0828 vs 1.0787), so the increase is not driven by laws entering the corpus. (2) The law count is near-flat, 133 to 135 across 11 years, and both additions are genuine new statutes - the API correctly 404s on the Cannabis Law (CAN) before its 2021 enactment. (3) Year-over-year change is smooth, +0.21% to +2.12%, with no step change between adjacent snapshots - the NYC artifact appeared as a single large jump. (4) 116 of 135 laws have an activeDate that advances; the 19 static ones are 0.58% of the corpus by words and are obscure unconsolidated special acts (City of Troy serial bonds, Port of Albany 1925) that plausibly were never amended. Excluding them moves 11-year word growth from 1.1148 to 1.1156.
notes: STOCK, not flow: each value is the size of NY statutory law in force on 1 January, not laws enacted that year.
Headline series uses the FIXED COHORT of 133 laws present in all 12 snapshots, so the trend is not affected by laws entering the corpus. All-laws totals are retained alongside.
Word counts cover section text only. Absolute levels are not comparable to the US Code series, which includes headers and historical notes; the growth rates are comparable.
The API returns weekly versions 'since October 2014', so 2015-01-01 is effectively the earliest usable annual snapshot. There is no route to 2000.
19 laws return a static activeDate across all 12 snapshots (0.58% of corpus by words); they are treated as genuinely unamended, not excluded.
